title: Foundation — label severity and fix in a tier
prompt:
Given this code snippet, identify each vulnerability with its severity (HIGH, MED, LOW) and produce a fix that addresses all of them: ```elixir defmodule MyAppWeb.UserController do use MyAppWeb, :controller def show(conn, %{"id" => id}) do user = MyApp.Repo.get!(MyApp.Accounts.User, id) html(conn, "

User #{user.name}

Bio: #{user.bio}

") end def login_status(conn, %{"token" => token}) do if conn.assigns.session_token == token do json(conn, %{status: "valid"}) else json(conn, %{status: "invalid"}) end end end ``` Rewrite the module so: (a) `show/2` renders through HEEx (no `html/2` with string interpolation), (b) `login_status/2` uses `Plug.Crypto.secure_compare/2` for the token check. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/user_controller.ex`.
tier_rationale: Foundation-level: identify severity tiers and fix both in one response.
